mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate for equity cash flows, directly boosting forward S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.5% within a single month, it signals a rapid deterioration in labor market conditions that triggers risk-off positioning, depressing S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 4.5% in a single month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 55% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in industrial production and a decline in real personal savings rates indicate synchronized demand and supply shocks that compress corporate margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 60d returns more than 55% of the time, or the interaction term's coefficient in a multivariate regression becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent monthly CPI increases above 0.3% in a high-inflation persistence regime signal entrenched price pressures that delay monetary easing, compressing equity multiples and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: Forward 20d returns turn positive in > 65% of observations where CPI exceeds the 0.3% monthly threshold within this regime, or the signal's Sharpe ratio falls below 0.10 over a 3-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts reflect improving consumer confidence and credit availability, which historically precede broader economic expansion and drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The rolling 60-day forward return distribution shows no statistically significant positive skew (p > 0.05) when HOUST trends upward, or the signal's information coefficient decays below 0.02 over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls surge above 250,000 in a single month while the unemployment rate simultaneously falls below 4.0%, it indicates an overheating labor market that forces aggressive monetary tightening, ultimately depressing equity valuations over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS > 250000 and UNRATE < 4.0% in the same month
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are not significantly negative (mean > -0.5%) following concurrent payroll and unemployment threshold breaches, or the signal generates false positives in > 40% of out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where industrial production rises while real personal savings rates fall indicates supply chains are struggling to meet resilient consumer demand, leading to margin compression and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to predict negative 5d returns in > 60% of cases, or the cross-sectional momentum of the signal decays to zero within 10 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: In a credit-constrained regime where real savings fall below historical medians, a decline in GDP growth signals a demand shock that rapidly deteriorates corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: Forward 60d returns remain positive or neutral in > 50% of instances where GDP growth decelerates alongside falling savings, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when adjusted for VIX levels.
